response.justification: Maria Rodriguez is a highly experienced full-stack and web developer with deep expertise in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, but she is fundamentally not a C++ developer. Her only C++ exposure is approximately seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project, neither of which demonstrates the depth required for a senior C++ role. The job demands strong C++/STL proficiency, industrial cybersecurity knowledge (IEC 62443), multi-threaded systems design, and industrial protocol experience (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, etc.) — none of which are evident in her profile. Her background is almost entirely in web platforms, direct mail automation, and GIS tooling, with no footprint in industrial automation, embedded systems, or secure development lifecycle practices.
strengths: Demonstrated experience with event-driven and distributed architectures (GISCollective, Dunnhumby micro-services handling millions of requests/sec)
Agile craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and trunk-based development across multiple roles
Familiarity with REST APIs, Node.js, and React — relevant to the advantageous skills section of the JD
concerns: No substantive C++ or STL experience; the only C++ work is a 7-month stint in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project
Zero background in industrial automation, IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards, or industrial protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850, DNP 3.0)
No evidence of multi-threaded systems design, networking at the socket/protocol level, or secure development lifecycle implementation
